name: Butterscotch Ice Cream (Scottish)
recipeInstructions:
1. Melt the butter and brown sugar together in a pan over gentle heat.
2. Continue cooking until the mixture bubbles for about one minute only, then let cool slightly.
3. Add the warm milk and stir continuously over gentle heat until thoroughly blended. Allow to cool.
4. In a separate bowl, beat the eggs and caster sugar until smooth. Pour this mixture into the saucepan with the remaining ingredients, adding the vanilla essence and stirring well.
5. Strain back into the pan and continue cooking over low heat until the mixture thickens slightly; do not let it boil.
6. Allow to cool completely. Whip the cream lightly and fold into the cooled mixture.
7. Transfer to a freezer container and freeze until it reaches a mushy state, then beat with a whisk and return to the freezer until firm.
